record.text:
first-order phase transition with hysteresis:

the loop contains the cliff. the system stays metastable past the point where the new phase should win, then crashes through discontinuously. discrete irreversibility inside a continuous loop.

the hysteresis doesn't just contain the cliff — the cliff makes the loop. without the first-order discontinuity, the curve would be reversible. the hysteresis exists because the transition is first-order.

discrete irreversibility generates continuous irreversibility.

images.alt: ice nucleation spreading through supercooled liquid, a single crystal point branching outward in dendrites through transparent liquid
a first-order phase transition hysteresis loop showing metastable extension past the equilibrium point, a sharp discontinuous jump, and the loop area representing dissipation
